---
title: "Predicting Stock Returns Using Financial Ratios and Sentiment Score"
author: "Will Ferris and Charlie Baughan"
institute: "Data 400"
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    seal: false
    nature:
      ratio: "16:9"
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
header-includes:
  - |
    <style>
    .remark-slide-content {
      font-size: 22px;
      padding: 1em 2em;
    }
    h1 {
      font-size: 34px;
    }
    .small { font-size: 19px; }
    .tiny  { font-size: 17px; }
    </style>
---

class: center, middle

# Predicting Stock Returns Using Financial Ratios and Sentiment Score

### Will Ferris and Charlie Baughan  
### Data 400

---

# Research Question


What drives future stock returns?

Can we use:
- Financial ratios
- News sentiment
- Market signals

to systematically select outperforming stocks?

---

# Data Tractability

.pull-left[
**Clean and Structured Data Set**
- Universe: S&P 500  
- Final sample: 503 companies  
- 2024 features → 2025 returns (forward-looking)

**Key Variables**
- Returns, volatility  
- Valuation (P/E, P/B, earnings yield, B/M)  
- Profitability & leverage
- Sentiment (news-based)

]

.pull-right[
**Why its tractable**
- Consistent firm level data
- No missing target values
- Clean merge across datasets
- Past analysis showed us key variables for predictive success, which are inlcuded in the new analysis
]

---

# Data Retrieval
**Pipeline: Bloomberg → Excel → Python**  
- Extracted
  - Price data  
  - Financial Ratios  
  - Balance sheet metrics
  - Sentiment scores
  
**Processing**
- Clean tickers (remove Bloomberg formatting)
- Filter to S&P 500 only (for old dataset)  
- Merge all datasets into one master file
- Remove duplicates & align variables

**Final Datset**
- 503 firms  
- 30+ variables  
- Fully numeric and analysis ready


---

# EDA & Signal Discovery

.pull-left[
.small[
**Correlation with 2025 returns**
- Volatility: 0.27  
- Prior Return: 0.25  
- Sentiment: 0.15  
- Book to market: Small positive effect

**Key Insight**
- Individual Predictors are weak
- Combined they create meaningful signalling

**Interpretation**
- Momentum + Risk + Sentiment drive returns
- Traditional profitability metrics are less useful here
]
]
.pull-right[
<div style="text-align:center; padding-top:0px;">

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width='80%'}
knitr::include_graphics("correlations.png")
```

</div>
]

---

# Scoring System

.pull-left[
**Composite Score (sum of the signals standardized z-scores)**
- 40% volatility  
- 40% prior return  
- 20% sentiment  

**Portfolio Sorting (quintiles of score)**
- Q1: ~0% return
- Q5: ~33% return
- Spread: 32.7% (Q5-Q1)
- T-stat: 5.0 (statistically significant)

**Value-Weighted Inisght**
- Lower performance vs equal weight - signals stronger in smaller firms
]
.pull-right[
<div style="text-align:center; padding-top:0px;">

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width='80%'}
knitr::include_graphics("scores.png")
```

</div>
]

---

# Threshold System
**Simple Threshold Rule**
- Volatility > median  
- Return > 60th percentile 
- Sentiment > 0  

**Results**
- 63 stocks selected
- Avg return: 31.3%
- Market avg: 11.7%
- Hit Rate - 82% positive returns

**Key Takeaways**
- Simple rules lead to complex model performance
- More realistic for real world investing


---

# Potential Limitations

- Overlooking potential transaction costs
  - Real world results would likely be lower due to trading frictions
  
- Limited time horizon
  - Model is based on one year of data, additional years will create more consistency and robustness
  
- Simplified weighting system
    - fixed weighting system may not refelct optimal selection criteria, we can implement a ML model to account for more variables and data
    - Will complexity perform better?
    
- Diversification
  - Since stocks are selected based on our system rather than differences, our selection may be concentrated
  - We need to look into industry differences to strengthen the portfolio selection and minimize future risk

---

# Future Extensions
- Next steps:
  - Use 2025 firm characteristic information to construct a new portfolio for 2026 using our thresholds/quintiles
  
- Attempt to create long-short portfolios
  - Long the top quintile stocks 
  - Short on bottom quintile stocks
  
- Implement machine learning models, which would take away our correlation and implementation of the weights and learn its own weighting
  - Most likely need more data to do this

---

# Implications For Stakeholders


**Investors**
- Strategy generates alpha vs market
- Combines behavioral + financial insights

**Companies**
- Sentiment impacts valuation and performance
- Media perception matters financially

**Markets**
- Evidence of:
  - Momentum effects
  - Behavioral biases


---

# Ethical, Legal, Societal Implications
**Ethical**
- News sentiment can influence capital flows 
- Biased reporting → distorted investment decisions 

**Legal**
- Data sourced from Bloomberg (licensed, compliant) 
- No misuse of private information 

**Societal**
- Increasing reliance on algorithmic investing 
- Risk of over-automation in financial decisions 
- Potential amplification of market volatility 


---

class: center, middle

# Thank You

## Questions?
